30.reason: [The count should be initialized to 1 after reading a complete word, not 0. Also, this assignment is misplaced鈥攊t runs before the full word is read.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [The count should be initialized to 1 after reading a complete word, not 0. Also, this assignment is misplaced鈥攊t runs before the full word is read.]

42.reason: [This increments from 0 to 1, which works, but the earlier initialization to 0 is confusing and error-prone. However, the real issue is that 'ch' after the inner while loop is the first non-alpha character, but the outer loop doesn't reprocess it, causing potential missed characters if multiple non-alpha chars appear. But more critically, the logic for reading words skips non-alpha correctly, so this may not be the main bug.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [This increments from 0 to 1, which works, but the earlier initialization to 0 is confusing and error-prone. However, the real issue is that 'ch' after the inner while loop is the first non-alpha character, but the outer loop doesn't reprocess it, causing potential missed characters if multiple non-alpha chars appear. But more critically, the logic for reading words skips non-alpha correctly, so this may not be the main bug.]

52.reason: [strcmp returns 0 for equal strings, <0 if first < second, >0 otherwise. Using ==1 is incorrect; it should be ==0 to check equality. This causes wrong grouping of identical words.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [strcmp returns 0 for equal strings, <0 if first < second, >0 otherwise. Using ==1 is incorrect; it should be ==0 to check equality. This causes wrong grouping of identical words.]

55.reason: [Same error: using ==1 instead of ==0 for string equality. This prevents proper aggregation of duplicate words.]
